headline: Salesforce Cuts 700 Jobs in Streamlining Effort, Yet Eyes Growth with 1,000 Open Roles
summary: Salesforce Inc Announces Layoffs Amid Investor Pressure: Salesforce Inc (NYSE: CRM) is reducing its workforce by 700 employees, a strategic move to increase margins under the influence of activist shareholders like Elliott Management. Despite this downsizing, Salesforce keeps 1,000 jobs open, indicating a recalibration rather than a major shift. This decision follows their 2023 reduction of 8,000 employees and aligns with the broader tech industry trend of AI-focused hiring and cutbacks in other areas.
